channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Special voter roll revision cuts electorate by a quarter in Mamata Banerjee’s Bhowanipore seat
item.description: The report explains how the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of the electoral roll has sharply reduced the number of registered voters in West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee’s Bhowanipore assem...

item.title: Macron Engages Lebanon in Efforts to Broaden U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Talks
item.description: During the ongoing 2026 conflict between the United States and Iran, French President Emmanuel Macron has intensified diplomatic efforts to help stabilize the broader Middle East region. Reports indic...

item.title: Australia leads joint call for Lebanon to be included in Middle East ceasefire and protection of aid workers
item.description: Australia has expressed deep concern over the worsening humanitarian situation in Lebanon and has led a joint statement with Brazil, Colombia, Indonesia, Jordan, Sierra Leone and the UK calling for th...

item.title: Review of Omah Lay Second Album 'Clarity of Mind'
item.description: Omah Lay don drop him second album wey im call 'Clarity of Mind' and e dey show say the Port Harcourt boy don grow well well for him music and life. Instead of the old depressive talk about mental hea...

item.title: Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz After Israeli Attacks On Lebanon
item.description: Tension don rise for Middle East as Iran don close the Strait of Hormuz following fresh Israeli attacks for Lebanon. This one happen just after United States, Iran, and Israel gree say make dem pause ...

item.title: Doug Allan, acclaimed wildlife cameraman who worked with David Attenborough, dies in Nepal aged 74
item.description: Doug Allan, the pioneering Scottish wildlife cameraman and photographer known for his work on landmark BBC natural history series, has died at the age of 74 while trekking in Nepal. Allan, who was pri...

item.title: Nigerian Army Rejects Claims on Soldier Welfare Made by Former Member
item.description: The Nigerian Army don respond to tori wey a former soldier, Rotimi Olamilekan, known as 'Soja Boi', don spread. For one viral interview, Olamilekan claim say soldiers dey face poor welfare, dey underp...

item.title: Astronauts witness meteorite impacts during Artemis II lunar flyby
item.description: During NASA's Artemis II mission, astronauts made history with a lunar flyby, becoming the first humans to travel around the moon in over 50 years. During their journey, they witnessed multiple meteor...

item.title: RSPCA rescues over 250 dogs from overcrowded property in the UK
item.description: In a recent multi-animal rescue operation, the RSPCA found more than 250 dogs at a property in an undisclosed location in the United Kingdom. Images shared by the charity showed dozens of dogs confine...

item.title: Prominent Nigerians Recognised at 2026 Nigeria Broadcasting Awards
item.description: For Abuja, the Broadcasting Organisations of Nigeria (BON) don hold the second edition of the Nigeria Broadcasting Awards (TNBA) wey celebrate excellence for broadcasting. Na big names like Governor D...

item.title: Nairobi Landlord Sentenced for Fatal Building Collapse in Huruma
item.description: A Nairobi landlord, Samuel Kamau Karanja, has been sentenced to seven years in prison for the 2013 collapse of a building in Ngei II Estate, Huruma, which claimed the lives of 52 tenants. The court fo...

item.title: US vice‑president JD Vance defends visit to Hungary ahead of tense election
item.description: US vice‑president JD Vance has strongly rejected accusations that Washington is interfering in Hungary’s forthcoming parliamentary election, calling the claims 'darkly ironic' as the opposition Tisza ...

item.title: Worker Arrested After Ontario Warehouse Fire Allegedly Shown in Self-Posted Video
item.description: Authorities in Ontario, California, have arrested 29-year-old Chamel Abdulkarim of Highland in connection with a massive fire that destroyed a 1.2-million-square-foot Kimberly-Clark paper products war...

item.title: U.S. State Department Issues Travel Advisory for Nepal Amid Natural Disasters and Protests
item.description: The U.S. State Department has updated its travel advisory for Americans planning trips to Nepal, reducing the warning level to 'Level 2: Exercise Increased Caution.' This follows the end of nationwide...

item.title: Affiliate Marketing: Demystifying Cost-Per-Action (CPA) Models
item.description: This article delves into the nuances of Cost-Per-Action (CPA) affiliate marketing, particularly relevant in a crowded digital landscape. It emphasizes the importance of understanding deal structures a...

item.title: Arne Slot remains hopeful for Liverpool’s Champions League comeback despite PSG defeat
item.description: Arne Slot is optimistic that Anfield can inspire Liverpool to mount another famous Champions League comeback after their 2-0 defeat to Paris Saint-Germain in the first leg of the quarter-finals. The R...

item.title: Israeli airstrikes target Hezbollah positions across Lebanon, resulting in significant casualties
item.description: On 8 April 2026, Israel conducted a large-scale wave of airstrikes across Lebanon, targeting what it described as Hezbollah military sites and command centres. The strikes hit areas including the sout...

item.title: Former MK Party secretary-general Bongani Mncwango joins IFP
item.description: Former MK Party secretary-general, Bongani Mncwango, has officially joined the Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) following his recent dismissal from the MK Party. Mncwango was replaced by Sibonelo Nomvalo a...

item.title: International mediators urge calm after reports of US-Iran ceasefire breaches
item.description: Diplomatic mediators have called for restraint from both the United States and Iran as reports of multiple violations to their recently established truce surface. According to AFP correspondents in va...

item.title: Golden Arrows coach Manqoba Mngqithi highlights Deon Hotto’s intensity after 5-0 defeat to Orlando Pirates
item.description: Golden Arrows coach Manqoba Mngqithi used the recent heavy 5-0 loss to Orlando Pirates as a teaching moment for his squad. Despite the drubbing at Orlando Amstel Arena on Tuesday, Mngqithi pointed to ...
